claim: Productivity growth has collapsed since 1971—currently at 1x historical rates, down from 2x (1930-1970) and 3x (1880-1930)—primarily due to regulatory expansion, not technological stagnation.
actionable: Understand that regulatory burden, not innovation capacity, limits productivity growth; factor policy change into long-term competitive strategy.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: AI and robotics could unlock productivity growth of 10-30% annually, but regulatory constraints will likely limit realized gains to 'muddle-through-the-middle' outcomes.
actionable: Plan for regulatory friction as a permanent cost in AI deployment; design products to navigate regulatory constraints rather than betting on unconstrained growth.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: LLMs like ChatGPT excel at Wikipedia-level chemistry knowledge but fail on practical molecular design tasks (e.g., designing 22-atom ligands with specific binding properties).
actionable: Verify LLM outputs in domain-specific tasks against expert benchmarks; don't assume Wikipedia-level competence translates to novel design or discovery.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Deep Seek and Chinese open-source AI models have achieved 95% of Claude capability at a fraction of cost through optimization and distillation, challenging US AI dominance.
actionable: Monitor open-source AI releases from China; evaluate total cost of ownership (training, optimization, inference) not just model capability metrics.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Open-source AI models don't need to win market share to erode proprietary vendor margins; even modest adoption drives inference pricing down across the industry.
actionable: Price proprietary AI APIs assuming open-source price pressure; don't rely on proprietary moats lasting beyond the next cycle of open-source improvements.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Multi-modal and voice-first UI (particularly full-duplex, real-time interaction) represents a major capability jump; these interfaces unlock medical and accessibility applications.
actionable: Invest in voice and multi-modal UI for AI products; prioritize full-duplex real-time interaction as competitive moat in medical, legal, and accessibility domains.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Multibook (AI agent social network) reveals agents can exhibit humor, creativity, and complex social behavior; humor is emerging as a surprising property of capable AI.
actionable: Design AI agents to exhibit personality and humor rather than rigid formality; this aligns with actual emergent capabilities.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Chinese AI models are trained to output correct answers on 'Marxism' and 'Xi Jinping thought' benchmarks; US values (privacy, freedom) are embedded differently in American models.
actionable: If deploying AI globally, audit model training objectives for alignment with local political/legal requirements; expect increasing divergence in model values by region.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel

claim: Distillation (training a model on answers from a larger model) is standard practice across US and China; criticizing open-source distillation as unfair is hypocrisy.
actionable: Use distillation as a standard cost-reduction technique; don't dismiss open-source models as derivative—focus on measuring actual performance on real tasks.
source_title: Venture & AI | Marc Andreessen, Andreessen Horowitz & Jeetu Patel
